turns.text: Thanks for calling Smith Plumbing, this is Ashley, how can I help you today?
Yes hello, I am calling because I have, uh, appointment for the... the pipe is block? The drain.
Sure thing, you have an appointment for a blocked drain. What can I help you with regarding that?
I need to, how you say, cancel it. I no need anymore. My neighbor, he fix for me.
Oh great, your neighbor fixed the blocked drain for you! Let me pull up your appointment. Can I get the name on the booking?
Yes, is under Minh Tran. T-R-A-N.
Got it, Minh Tran. And do you remember what day that appointment was scheduled for?
Uh, I think is Thursday? This Thursday, yes.
Okay, I see your Thursday appointment right here. I'll go ahead and cancel that for you, no problem at all.
Thank you so much. If it come back, the block, I call again yes?
Absolutely, if the blockage comes back just give us a call and we'll get you taken care of. Have a great day, Minh!
Okay, thank you, bye bye.
Hi there, you've reached Smith Plumbing! This is Jordan, how can I help?
Um, hi... I think I have a problem with my, uh, I'm not sure what it's called. The water won't go down?
Sounds like the water won't go down, so that's probably a clogged drain. Is it in your kitchen or bathroom?
It's... well, it's kind of in both? Or maybe just the one in the... wait, is it the sink or the tub? I think the tub.
Okay, so your bathtub drain isn't draining. We can definitely help with that!
But actually, I'm not sure if I should call a plumber or if this is more of like a... do I need a different kind of person? Like a pipe person?
Oh no worries, a plumber is exactly the right person for a clogged tub drain! That's what we do.
Oh okay, that's good I guess. Um, how much would something like that... actually hold on, someone's at my door. Can you just--
Sure, take your time!
Hello? ... Are you still there? ... Alright, well if you need us, don't hesitate to call back. Bye now.
Good morning, Smith Plumbing, this is Taylor speaking. How can I help you?
Hi Taylor, I've got a leak under my kitchen sink. It's been dripping for a couple days now.
Oh no, a leak under the kitchen sink for a couple days. Let me see what we have available to get someone out there for you.
Actually, you know what, I think I'm going to try to handle it myself first. I watched a YouTube video and it doesn't look too hard.
Totally understand! If it's a simple drip, sometimes tightening the connections or replacing a washer can do the trick.
Yeah that's what the video said. But, hmm... actually, what would it cost to have someone come look at it? Just in case I mess it up.
Our service call is $89, and that includes the diagnosis. Any repairs would be quoted on-site before we start.
Eighty-nine dollars... you know what, let me just go ahead and book it. I don't want to make it worse.
Smart thinking! We've got an opening this Friday at 10 AM, does that work for you?
Friday at 10 works great.
Perfect. Can I get your name and a good phone number?
Rachel Kim, and my number is 503-555-7821.
Got it, Rachel. You're all set for Friday at 10 AM for that kitchen sink leak. We'll give you a call when the tech is on the way.
Awesome, thank you so much Taylor.
You're welcome, Rachel! Talk to you Friday.
Smith Plumbing, this is Casey. What can I do for you today?

Oh hi Casey! So glad I got a real person, you know how it is with those automated systems, press one for this, press two for that, drives me crazy. Anyway! I've got this little drip situation happening.
Ha, I hear you on the phone trees! So tell me about this drip you've got going on.

Right, so it's my bathroom faucet, the hot water side. It just kind of drips, drips, drips. Reminds me of when my dad's house had the same thing, must've been, oh, twenty years ago? They replaced the whole faucet but I'm hoping I don't need all that.
Oh a dripping bathroom faucet on the hot side, that's usually a pretty easy fix actually. Have you tried replacing the washer or the cartridge inside the handle?
The cartridge? Like a printer cartridge? Ha, just kidding. No I haven't tried anything, I'm not very handy. My husband usually does stuff like this but he's out of town visiting his sister in Tucson.
Ha! Well here's a tip -- if it's a single-handle faucet, you can usually pop off the cap, unscrew the handle, and swap the cartridge for about ten bucks from the hardware store.
Oh really? Ten bucks? That's way better than a service call. Is there a video or something you'd recommend?
Just search your faucet brand plus "cartridge replacement" on YouTube and you'll find a ton of walkthroughs. If it doesn't work out, give us a ring and we'll send someone right over.
You know what, I'm going to try that! You're so sweet for helping me save some money. If I mess it up I'll be calling you right back, ha!
Happy to help! Good luck with it, and we're here if you need us.
Thanks Casey, you have a wonderful day! Bye!
Thank you for calling Smith Plumbing, this is Morgan. How can I help?
Yeah, I need someone out here NOW. My water heater is making this horrible banging noise and there's no hot water. I've been dealing with cold showers for two days!
Oh no, two days of cold showers and a banging water heater, that sounds awful. Let me check our schedule right away.
Please do, because this is ridiculous. I called another company and they blew me off.
I'm so sorry to hear that. Unfortunately, I'm looking at our schedule and we're completely booked through the end of this week.
You're kidding me. You're booked too? What am I supposed to do, just not have hot water?
I completely understand your frustration. The earliest I can get someone out is Monday morning at 8 AM -- and I can put you first on our cancellation list in case something opens up sooner.
Monday?! That's five more days! This is unbelievable.
I know, and I'm really sorry. If it helps, I can also give you a couple other companies to try in case they have availability this week.
Fine. Yeah, give me the names I guess. And put me on that cancellation list too.
Absolutely. Can I get your name and number for the cancellation list?
It's Marcus Jefferson, 312-555-4490.
Got it, Marcus. You're on the list, and I'd also try Downtown Plumbing at 312-555-2200 and Ace Pipe Works at 312-555-3310. I hope you get it sorted soon.
Yeah, thanks I guess. Bye.
Hi, you've reached Smith Plumbing! This is Sam, how can I help you today?
Oh hello dear. Yes, I'm calling because my water heater, it's... well, the water just doesn't seem to get as hot as it used to. Takes forever to warm up.
I see, so your water heater isn't getting as hot as it used to and it's taking longer to warm up. How old is the unit, do you know?
Oh goodness, let me think. We had it put in when... it must have been, oh, maybe 2010? 2011? My husband would have known, God rest him.
I'm sorry to hear that. So it's about 15 years old then. At that age, water heaters do start to lose efficiency.
Oh my. So does that mean I need a whole new one? That sounds expensive. I'm on a fixed income, you see.
Not necessarily! Sometimes it just needs the sediment flushed out or the heating element replaced, which is a lot less than a full replacement.
Sediment, you said? Is that like... dirt inside the tank?
Exactly, minerals build up over time at the bottom of the tank and make it harder for the heater to do its job. A flush can really help.
Oh, well that's good to know. Let me think about it and maybe talk to my son first. He likes to be involved with these decisions. Can I call back?
Of course, take your time! We're here Monday through Saturday, 8 to 5. Just call whenever you're ready.
Thank you so much dear, you've been very helpful. Have a lovely day.
You too, take care now!
Smith Plumbing, this is Dana. How can I help you?
Look, my toilet has been running nonstop for three days and it's driving me insane. The noise, the water bill -- I need this fixed yesterday.
Oh man, a toilet running nonstop for three days, I totally get why you're frustrated. Let's get you taken care of.
I hope so because I am at my wit's end with this thing.
Let me check our schedule right now. Looks like we've got an opening tomorrow at 1 PM, would that work?
Tomorrow? Can't you do today?
Let me see... I actually have a 4:30 slot today if you can make that work.
Yes. 4:30 today. I'll be here.
Great, 4:30 it is. Can I get your name and phone number?
Darnell Washington, 678-555-1134.
Got it, Darnell. And what's the address?
842 Maple Lane.
Perfect. Darnell Washington, 842 Maple Lane, today at 4:30 for that running toilet. Our tech will call you about 30 minutes before arrival.
Alright, thank you. I appreciate you squeezing me in.
Of course, we'll get that toilet sorted out for you. See you this afternoon!
Good afternoon, Smith Plumbing! This is Riley, how can I help?
Hi Riley, my toilet keeps running after I flush. It eventually stops but it takes like five minutes.
Oh that's annoying, a toilet that keeps running for five minutes after each flush. That's usually the flapper valve.
The flapper? Is that something I'd need a plumber for?

Honestly, it's one of the easiest DIY fixes out there! You can grab a universal flapper at any hardware store for about five bucks. You just unhook the old one and snap the new one on, no tools needed.
Oh really? That sounds simple enough. How do I know it's the flapper though?
Try this -- put a few drops of food coloring in the tank. If the color seeps into the bowl without flushing, your flapper isn't sealing right.
That's a great tip, I'll try that tonight. And if it's not the flapper?
Then give us a call back and we'll send someone out to diagnose it properly. But nine times out of ten, it's the flapper.
Awesome, I'll give it a shot first. Thanks Riley, that's really helpful.
Happy to help! Good luck, and we're here if you need us.
Smith Plumbing, this is Jamie, how can I help?
Hey, I need to cancel an appointment. Quick, I'm in a meeting.
Sure thing, I can take care of that quick. What name is it under?
Priya Patel. Faucet install, Wednesday.
Got it, Priya Patel, faucet install on Wednesday. I'll cancel that right now.
Great, thanks.
Done! Want me to call you if we get any openings for a reschedule?
No, I'm going with someone else. Thanks though, bye.
No problem at all, take care Priya!
Hi, Smith Plumbing! This is Alex, what can I do for you?
Hey Alex! So I just bought this gorgeous new kitchen faucet, it's one of those pull-down sprayer ones, you know the kind? Matte black, very modern. My wife picked it out, she has great taste.
Oh nice, those matte black pull-down faucets look amazing! Are you looking to have it installed?

Well, that's the thing. I was going to install it myself -- I've done some projects around the house, built a deck last summer actually -- but then I thought, eh, plumbing is different. But also I hate spending money on stuff I can probably figure out.
I hear you, if you built a deck you've definitely got some skills! Faucet installs can be tricky though, especially under the sink.
Right, and my back isn't what it used to be. Plus last time I did plumbing I flooded the bathroom, ha! My wife still brings that up at parties. Okay you know what, what would it cost?
For a faucet install it's $150 flat, parts not included since you already have the faucet.
A hundred fifty? Hm. That's... yeah, okay. Let's do it. Save my back and my marriage, right?
Ha! Smart move. How does next Tuesday at 11 sound?
Tuesday at 11 is perfect, I work from home so I'm always around. Well, I'm supposed to be working. Don't tell my boss.
Your secret's safe with me! Can I get your name and number?
You're all set Greg, next Tuesday at 11 AM for the faucet install. Our tech will call before heading over.
Awesome, you guys are great. My wife is going to be so happy. Alright, talk soon!
Looking forward to it! Have a great rest of your day, Greg.
Smith Plumbing, this is Parker. How can I help?
I've got sewage backing up into my basement right now. Actual sewage. This is an emergency!
Oh no, sewage in your basement -- that's definitely an emergency. Let me get our on-call tech on the line right away.
Please! It's getting worse by the minute. I've got two kids in this house and it smells horrible.
I hear you, with kids in the house that's a health concern. Can I get your name, address, and number real quick so I can dispatch someone immediately?
Tony Russo, 1547 Birch Court. 215-555-6643.
Got it, Tony. I'm radioing our emergency tech right now. In the meantime, if you can, try to avoid running any water in the house.
Yeah we already shut everything off. How long until someone gets here?
Our emergency tech is about 25 minutes out. He'll call you when he's five minutes away.
Twenty-five minutes. Okay. Fine. Just please hurry.
He's on his way, Tony. Hang tight, we're going to get this handled for you.
Alright, thank you.
Thanks for calling Smith Plumbing! This is Robin, how can I help?
Hello, yes, I have problem with the, uh, the big pipe? Under the ground? The smell is very bad outside.
Okay, so you're smelling something bad outside near the big underground pipe -- that sounds like it could be your sewer line. Is that right?
Yes, yes, sewer. That is the word. Sewer line. The smell come from the ground, near the... how you say, the grass area.
Got it, a bad smell coming from the ground near your yard, probably a sewer line issue. Let me check when we can get someone out there.
Okay, yes please. Is very bad smell, the neighbor complain also.
I'm sorry, that must be really unpleasant with the neighbors complaining too. Unfortunately we're fully booked this week and next week.
Two week? Oh no, this is too long. The smell, it is every day worse.
I understand, two weeks is a long time with that kind of smell. I can put you on our waitlist and also recommend another company that might be able to come sooner.
Yes, other company is okay. Please give me the number.
Sure thing. Try Rivera Plumbing at 847-555-9100, they do sewer line work too. And what's your name and number for our waitlist?
Fatima Al-Rashid. My number is 847-555-3267.
Fatima, you're on the list. If anything opens up we'll call you right away. I hope Rivera can help you sooner!
Thank you very much. You are very kind. Goodbye.
Smith Plumbing, this is Drew, how can I help?
Garbage disposal is dead. Need someone to replace it. What's your soonest?
Dead garbage disposal, got it. I've got Thursday at 9 AM open, does that work?
Thursday 9 works. How much?
It's $175 for labor plus the cost of the unit, which depends on the model. Usually runs $120 to $250.
Fine. Book it.
Done. Name and phone number?
Jake Nguyen, 512-555-0487.
Jake Nguyen, Thursday at 9 AM for the garbage disposal replacement. We'll call before we head over.
Great, thanks.
See you Thursday, Jake!
Hi there, Smith Plumbing! This is Skyler, how can I help you today?
Oh hello. Yes, I'm calling about my garbage disposal. It just makes a humming sound when I turn it on but nothing happens. It doesn't... spin or whatever it does.
A humming sound but it's not spinning -- that usually means something is jammed in there. Have you tried pressing the reset button on the bottom of the unit?
There's a reset button? I didn't know that. Where would that be exactly?
If you look underneath the disposal unit, right on the bottom, there's usually a small red or black button. Just push it in and see if that helps.
Oh, under the sink you mean? Let me... well, it's hard for me to get down there these days with my knees. Would a plumber need to come look at it?
They certainly can! But if you have someone who can peek under there for you, it might save you a service call.
My granddaughter is coming by this weekend. Maybe I'll have her try that button first. And if it still hums?
If the reset doesn't fix it, there might be something stuck inside. Just give us a call and we'll send someone out to take a look.
Alright dear, I'll try that first. You've been so patient with me. Thank you.
Not at all, you're a pleasure to talk to! Good luck this weekend, and call us back anytime.
I will. Bless you, bye now.
Smith Plumbing, this is Morgan. How can I help you?
Hi, I've got a burst pipe in my upstairs bathroom and water is pouring through the ceiling into my living room.
Oh no, a burst pipe coming through your ceiling -- that's an emergency. First thing, can you shut off your main water valve?
Yeah, I just turned it off. The water stopped but there's a lot of damage already.
Good, you did the right thing shutting it off. Let me get someone out to you right away. What's your name and address?
Lisa Chen, 2280 Oakwood Drive. My number is 916-555-7190.
Lisa Chen, 2280 Oakwood Drive. I'm dispatching our emergency plumber now, he should be there within 30 minutes.
Thirty minutes, okay. Is there anything else I should do in the meantime?
If you can, put some towels down and move any electronics or valuables away from the wet area. Our tech will handle the rest.
Already on it. Thank you so much for the quick response.
Of course, Lisa! Our tech Kevin will call when he's close. Hang in there!
Smith Plumbing, this is Taylor. How can I help?
Yeah I've got water everywhere, pipe burst under my kitchen. This is the third time this year something has gone wrong in this stupid house!
Oh no, water everywhere from a burst pipe, and the third issue this year -- I'm so sorry. Have you been able to shut off the water?
I can't find the shutoff valve! My husband installed some weird setup when he redid the kitchen and I have no idea where anything is!
Okay, check near your water meter or in the basement -- it's usually a red or blue handle on the main line coming in.
I'm looking, I'm looking... I don't see anything! There's water all over my hardwood floors, these floors cost us a fortune, this is--
I understand, your floors are getting damaged. Let me get our emergency plumber headed your way right now while you look. What's your address?
It's 405 -- oh God the water is going into the hallway now. I gotta go, I'll call back!
Wait, if you can give me -- ... hello? I hope she calls back. We'll be here.
Smith Plumbing, this is Jordan. What can I do for you?
Kitchen drain is clogged. Need someone today or tomorrow. What do you have?
Clogged kitchen drain, let me check today and tomorrow for you. One second.
Yep.
So unfortunately we're completely full today and tomorrow. The earliest I have is next Monday at 2 PM.
Monday? No. That doesn't work. I've got tenants complaining, I need this done fast.
I totally understand, tenants waiting with a clogged drain is no good. I wish I could squeeze you in but we're wall to wall.
Alright, who else does drain work around here?
Try Quick Flow Plumbing, 773-555-4500. They're good with drains and might have same-day availability.
Got it. Thanks.
Good luck, and call us anytime!
Good morning, Smith Plumbing! This is Ashley, how can I help you?
Hello dear, I'm calling because I've got a leak. It's in the bathroom, under the sink. I noticed a little puddle this morning when I was getting my towels.
Oh no, a puddle under the bathroom sink when you were getting your towels. How big of a leak are we talking?
Well it's not gushing or anything, just a small puddle. But I put a bowl under there for now. I don't want it to get worse, you know.
Smart to put a bowl under there! A small leak like that is usually a straightforward fix. Let me find a good time to get someone out to you.
That would be lovely. I'm home most days, so whenever is fine really. I don't go out much anymore.
How about Wednesday morning at 10? That way it's nice and early and you'll have the rest of your day.
Wednesday at 10, let me write that down... okay, Wednesday, 10 o'clock. Perfect.
Wonderful. And can I get your name and phone number, please?
Of course. It's Dorothy Hawkins. My number is... let me think... 614-555-2039. Did you get that?
Got it, Dorothy Hawkins, 614-555-2039. And what's your address?
129 Elm Street, Apartment 3B. It's the brick building on the corner.
Perfect, Dorothy. You're booked for Wednesday at 10 AM at 129 Elm Street, Apartment 3B, for that bathroom sink leak. Our plumber will give you a call before heading over.
Oh wonderful. Thank you so much, Ashley, you've been just delightful.
Aw, thank you Dorothy! We'll see you Wednesday morning. Take care!
You too, dear. Goodbye now.
Smith Plumbing, this is Casey! How can I help you today?
Um, hi. So my... I think it's the water heater? The water is kind of, like, not really hot but not cold either? It's just... lukewarm?
Lukewarm water instead of hot -- yep, that does sound like a water heater issue. Is it like that from every faucet or just one?
I... honestly I only tried the shower? Should I check the others? I don't really know how this stuff works.
No worries! Try running the hot water in your kitchen sink for a minute. If it's lukewarm there too, it's definitely the water heater.
Okay, yeah, I just tried and it's lukewarm everywhere. So what does that mean? Is it broken?
Not necessarily broken! Here's something easy to check first -- your water heater has a temperature dial on it, usually on the front. It might have gotten bumped to a lower setting.
A temperature dial? Like... on the tank thing? The big cylinder in my closet?
That's the one! Look for a knob or dial, it usually has settings like "warm," "hot," and "very hot." Try turning it up to hot and give it an hour to heat up.
Oh! Okay, I think I see it. It says... it's on "warm" right now. I bet my roommate changed it. Should I just turn it to hot?
Yep, turn it to hot and wait about an hour. That should do the trick! If it's still lukewarm after that, give us a call.
Oh man, I feel kind of silly now. Thank you though, that's really helpful!
Don't feel silly at all, it happens more than you'd think! Good luck, and we're here if you need us.
Smith Plumbing, this is Sam. How can I help?
Hello, please, I need help very fast. My toilet, the water, it go everywhere on the floor. It no stop!
Oh no, water going everywhere from the toilet and it won't stop -- that's an emergency. Is the water clean or dirty?
It is dirty, very dirty. It smell bad. I have towel but it keep coming. I don't know what to do!
Okay, there should be a small valve near the floor behind the toilet -- try turning it clockwise to shut off the water.
Behind the... okay, I look... I see small, uh, like a handle? I turn it?
Yes, turn that handle to the right, clockwise. That should stop the water from flowing.
Okay, okay I turn... yes! The water stop! Oh thank God. But the floor is very bad. Very mess.
Good, you stopped the water! Now let me get an emergency plumber to you right away. What's your name and address?
My name is Yuki Tanaka. I live at 1835 Pine Street, apartment number 7.
My phone is 206-555-3498.
Yuki Tanaka, 1835 Pine Street, Apartment 7. I'm sending our emergency tech now, he should be there in about 20 minutes.
Twenty minute, okay. I wait. Thank you, thank you so much.
You're welcome, Yuki. Keep that valve turned off and our tech will take care of everything. He'll call when he's close!
